title: papaver × bourgeauanum
intro: Papaver × bourgeauanum, also known as the Himalayan poppy, is a horticultural hybrid of Papaver nudicaule and P. alpinum, resulting in a unique and ornamental plant species. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Papaver × bourgeauanum include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an expectorant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Papaver × bourgeauanum is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, and it is also used as a decorative plant in gardens and floral arrangements.
